title: Smoky Chipotle Korean Chicken Stew
subtitle: Korean-Mexican Fusion
description: A smoky Korean-Mexican fusion braise with chipotle, gochujang, fire roasted tomatoes, and daikon — finished with lime and served as fried rice with a cracked egg.

ingredients.name: boneless skinless chicken thighs, cut into chunks
medium daikon radish, peeled and cubed
fire roasted diced tomatoes (half a 14oz can)
eggs, cracked into pan at serving time

steps.title: Cook and refrigerate rice
steps.text: Cook 1 cup of white rice according to package instructions. Spread out on a sheet pan to cool completely, then refrigerate — ideally overnight.
steps.note: Cold, dry rice is essential for the fried rice step — it fries without going mushy.

steps.title: Bloom aromatics and spices
steps.text: Turn the Instant Pot to Sauté and heat sesame oil (½ tbsp). Add:
steps.note: Cook about 2 minutes until fragrant and spices have bloomed in the oil.

steps.title: Sear chicken
steps.text: Add the chicken thighs (2.3 lb) and sear for 2–3 minutes, stirring a few times.

steps.title: Build the stew
steps.text: Pour in chicken broth (½ cup) and scrape up anything stuck on the bottom. Stir in:

steps.title: Pressure cook
steps.text: Lock the lid and pressure cook on High for 8 minutes. Let pressure naturally release for 5 minutes, then quick release the rest.

steps.title: Reduce the sauce
steps.text: Turn Sauté back on and simmer uncovered for 7–9 minutes, stirring occasionally. The fire roasted tomatoes help the sauce thicken faster — watch for a thick, glossy consistency.

steps.title: The finisher
steps.text: Turn the Instant Pot completely off. While still piping hot, stir in:
steps.note: The lime is essential here — the smoky braise needs that acid to cut through.

steps.title: Pack the containers
steps.text: Divide cold rice across 3 containers. Spoon stew equally over the rice. Top each with:
steps.note: Seal immediately and refrigerate or freeze.

steps.title: Fried rice at serving time
steps.text: Heat a pan on medium-high with a little neutral oil. Add one portion of cold rice and stew together and press down slightly. Fry until the bottom starts to crisp — about 3 minutes. Make a well in the center, crack in 1–2 eggs, let them set halfway, then fold everything together loosely. Top with fresh kimchi and serve immediately.
steps.note: Don't microwave this one — the fried rice method is the move.

notes.title: Chipotle heat level
notes.body: 2 chipotles is medium-smoky. Go 1 for mild, 3 for aggressive. The adobo sauce carries a lot of flavor on its own — don't skip it.

notes.title: Fire roasted tomatoes
notes.body: Just charred canned tomatoes — no oil, totally different from sun dried. They melt into the sauce adding body, acidity, and charred sweetness.

notes.title: The fried rice move
notes.body: Heat pan on medium-high, add oil, add cold rice and stew together and press down. Let the bottom crisp for 3 minutes. Make a well, crack in eggs, let them set halfway, then fold everything together.

notes.title: Storage
notes.body: Fridge up to 4 days, freezer up to 3 months. The fried rice step happens fresh at serving time — don't pack it already fried.
